stdout:
[36m[Claude][0m Hold for Claude...


[36m[Claude][0m Hey, I'm Claude — an AI assistant built into this meeting. I can look up issues, PRs, and project status on the fly, or answer questions about your codebase.


[36m[Claude][0m Let me check the captions quickly.


[36m[Claude][0m I don't see a name in the recent captions — the last 20 entries don't show anyone introducing themselves. What's your name? I'll pick it up and use it.


[36m[Claude][0m Pulling the last 5 minutes of captions.


[36m[Claude][0m Main threads from the last 5 minutes:


[36m[Claude][0m Marker file vs env var — Alice and Dan landed on marker file as the right call because codex starts the transcript server before the meeting URL exists, so the path can't be in the spawn args.


[36m[Claude][0m Testing gaps acknowledged — Bob and Carol flagged that the live Meet path (caption observer, meet.new, auto-leave) is being skipped this phase, but argued the risk is bounded since those code paths weren't touched.


[36m[Claude][0m Open items surfacing: Sentry alerts on the auth path, a leaked token that still needs revocation, byte ceiling on tool results to verify, a flaky test in the chat hardening suite, MCP startup latency, and the launch blocked on codex parity. Migration timeline is slipping. Rollout may get narrowed to 10% first — Priya has a concern about the approval policy there. Mohammed pinged about the database pick.


[36m[Claude][0m Wins: prompt cache hit rate looked good, wizard re-import is now idempotent, new Sonnet benchmarks strong on coding tasks.


[36m[Claude][0m One thing, said repeatedly by Alice, Dan, Carol, and Eve across the whole session: Sentry alerts are firing on the auth path. No one dug into root cause or next steps in the transcript — it kept surfacing as an open flag without resolution.


[36m[Claude][0m Five speakers: Dan, Eve, Carol, Alice, and Bob — all still active, each last spoke about a minute ago.


[36m[Claude][0m Three main threads in that window:


[36m[Claude][0m Byte ceiling risk — Bob and Alice worked through how the ceiling interacts with the model retrying on truncation, flagging a potential thrashing loop where it keeps hitting the cap without narrowing the filter.


[36m[Claude][0m Marker file rationale — Carol and Dan explained why the marker file wins over an env var: codex boots the transcript server before the meeting URL is known, so the path can't be in the spawn args.


[36m[Claude][0m Testing scope — Dan, Bob, and Eve openly acknowledged skipping the live Meet test run, arguing the risk is bounded since the affected paths weren't changed this phase.


[36m[Claude][0m Mohammed also pinged about the database pick somewhere in there, with no resolution visible in this window.


[36m[Claude][0m Yes — Carol, Dan, Bob, and Eve all mentioned it, with the same message each time: the launch is blocked on the codex parity work. No one offered a timeline or next step in these excerpts.


[36m[Claude][0m Let me grab the earliest part of the meeting to round out what I already have from the recent queries.


[36m[Claude][0m The same core themes ran throughout — this was a status/planning session. Here's the full picture:


[36m[Claude][0m Decisions made: Marker file wins over env var for the transcript server startup path — codex boots before the meeting URL exists, so the path can't be in the spawn args. Wizard re-import flow is confirmed idempotent. Database split is Postgres for prod, SQLite for tests.


[36m[Claude][0m Launch status: Blocked on codex parity work. Migration timeline is slipping. Rollout narrowing to 10% is on the table but not decided — Priya has a concern about the approval policy there.


[36m[Claude][0m Open problems: Sentry alerts firing on the auth path (flagged repeatedly, no owner or fix landed). Leaked token still needs to be revoked. MCP startup latency is acting up. Byte ceiling on tool results needs a check — Bob and Carol flagged a potential thrashing loop if the model retries on truncation without narrowing the filter. QA found a flaky test in the chat hardening suite.


[36m[Claude][0m Testing gap acknowledged: Live Meet path (caption observer, meet.new resolution, auto-leave) is being skipped this phase. Dan, Bob, Eve all agreed the risk is bounded since those paths weren't touched.


[36m[Claude][0m Coordination needed: Mobile release branch, and Mohammed pinged about the database pick with no response visible.


[36m[Claude][0m Good news: Prompt cache hit rate was strong, and the new Sonnet benchmarks looked good on coding tasks.


[36m[Claude][0m The only direct quotes on the database are these two lines:


[36m[Claude][0m Bob [09:47:28]: "Mohammed pinged about the database pick"


[36m[Claude][0m Carol [09:47:37]: "Postgres for prod, SQLite for tests is the standard split"


[36m[Claude][0m That's it — the database pick came up repeatedly as a flag (Mohammed pinged) but no one dug into it on the call.
